title: Plesioneuron excisum
taxonomy: Plesioneuron excisum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the Pacific Islands, specifically in New Caledonia. The classification of Plesioneuron excisum includes its placement in the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of vascular plants commonly known as the polypody ferns. Within the family Polypodiaceae, Plesioneuron excisum is placed in the genus Plesioneuron. Plesioneuron excisum is a member of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises of all vascular plants that produce spores.

intro: Plesioneuron excisum, also known as the Plesioneuron fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
